title: Out Over The Forth.
content:
Tune - "Charlie Gordon's welcome hame."


I.

        Out over the Forth I look to the north,
            But what is the north and its Highlands to me?
        The south nor the east gie ease to my breast,
            The far foreign land, or the wild rolling sea.

II.

        But I look to the west, when I gae to rest,
            That happy my dreams and my slumbers may be;
        For far in the west lives he I Io'e best,
            The lad that is dear to my babie and me.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Out Over The Forth."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
